§6Sour Gas§f

§aA corrosive byproduct of hydrodesulfurization — your most reliable source of sulfuric acid!§f

Sour Gas is a corrosive, flammable, polluting gas produced as a byproduct of late-game petroleum processing. It is obtained from the §bVacuum Refinery§f (when using regular crude oil) and from the §bHydrotreater§f (when desulfurizing various oils). It provides §e250 kTU§f per bucket and is one of the most reliable sources of §bSulfuric Acid§f in the game.

§6Production§f:
§b• Vacuum Refinery§f (Crude Oil @ 2 PU): §e150mB§f per §e1,000mB§f.
§b• Hydrotreater§f: Byproduct from treating Crude Oil, Cracked Oil, Diesel, Natural Gas, Coal Oil, or Cracked Diesel (usually §e150-300mB§f per §e1,000mB§f input).

§6Uses§f:
§b• Catalytic Reformer§f: §e1,000mB§f Sour Gas → §e750mB§f Sulfuric Acid + §e100mB§f Petroleum Gas + §e150mB§f Liquid Hydrogen (the primary use!).
§b• Coker Unit§f: §e250mB§f Sour Gas → §e1×§f Sulfur + §e150mB§f Coker Gas.
§b• Chemical Plant§f (TATB): §e200mB§f Sour Gas (at 1 PU) + §e10mB§f Nitric Acid + TNT → §e1×§f TATB.
§b• Industrial Solidification§f: §e7,200mB§f → §e1×§f Solid Fuel.
§b• Pyrolysis Oven§f: §e2,800mB§f → §e1×§f Solid Fuel.

§eTip§f: Sour Gas is both a §bcorrosive§f and a §btoxic§f gas — do not store it in unsealed containers! Convert it to §bSulfuric Acid§f via the Catalytic Reformer for safe storage and use. The acid is essential for fracking solution production and bedrock ore processing.
